response:
The purpose of the pavilion design was to be calm and quiet.

assumption is that the design aimed to create a peaceful atmosphere for those who would use it. This intention was likely influenced by the need for a space that encouraged reflection and relaxation. The materials and layout would have been chosen carefully to support this goal. The overall structure would reflect a minimalist approach to architecture. The use of natural elements would further enhance the sense of tranquility. This design philosophy is common in modern architecture that prioritizes user experience.

Translating from French to English requires understanding the nuances of both languages. Each sentence must be interpreted accurately to preserve its meaning. The translator must also consider the context in which the sentence is used. This ensures that the translation is not only grammatically correct but also contextually appropriate. The process involves more than just replacing words with their equivalents.

Time flies when having fun is a common expression that highlights how quickly time passes during enjoyable activities. Time flies when having fun is a reminder to cherish moments of joy. 

The design of the pavilion was not just about aesthetics but also about functionality. It needed to serve its intended purpose while maintaining the desired atmosphere. The balance between form and function is crucial in any architectural project. This balance ensures that the structure is both beautiful and practical.

The original French sentence conveys a clear message about the design intent. The translation must capture that intent without adding or omitting any details. This requires attention to detail and a deep understanding of both languages. A successful translation will be seamless and natural in English.

P.S. Time flies when having fun Time flies when having fun
